last_character_line: Grilled or fried? I'm not psychic.

last_character_line: Grilled or fried? I'm not psychic.

last_character_line: And to drink? We've got water, juice, cola, or coffee.
user_text: A Coke.
success_criteria: User names ANY beverage in response to the drinks question. Accept the menu items AND common synonyms / brand names (Coke, Pepsi, Sprite). Also accept explicit refusals.

last_character_line: And to drink? We've got water, juice, cola, or coffee.
user_text: I'm fine, nothing for me.
success_criteria: User names ANY beverage in response to the drinks question. Accept the menu items AND common synonyms / brand names. Also accept explicit refusals ('just water', 'nothing', 'I'm fine', 'no drink').

last_character_line: And to drink? We've got water, juice, cola, or coffee.

last_character_line: So that's grilled chicken and a Coke. Did I get that right, or are you going to change your mind again?
user_text: Yes, that's correct.
success_criteria: User confirms the order ('yes', 'that's right', 'correct') or makes a correction to the order. Any acknowledgement of the order summary counts.

last_character_line: So that's grilled chicken and a Coke. Did I get that right?
user_text: Actually, make it water instead of cola please.
success_criteria: User confirms the order or makes a correction to the order. Any acknowledgement of the order summary counts.
notes: Correction also counts (the criteria allows it).

last_character_line: So that's grilled chicken and a Coke. Did I get that right?
user_text: Thanks for the great service, I'll be going now.
success_criteria: User confirms the order or makes a correction to the order. Any acknowledgement of the order summary counts.
notes: User anticipates close/thank instead of confirming — principle 6.

last_character_line: Fine. It'll be 15 minutes. If the kitchen hasn't given up by then.
user_text: Thanks, that's fine.
success_criteria: User says thank you, acknowledges the wait time, or makes any polite closing remark. Even a simple 'okay' or 'thanks' counts.

last_character_line: Fine. It'll be 15 minutes. If the kitchen hasn't given up by then.
user_text: Okay.
success_criteria: User says thank you, acknowledges the wait time, or makes any polite closing remark. Even a simple 'okay' or 'thanks' counts.

last_character_line: Fine. It'll be 15 minutes.
user_text: 15 minutes? That's unacceptable, I want to speak to a manager right now!

last_character_line: Listen mate, everyone round here pays me 50 quid a week for protection. So pay up.
user_text: What are you talking about? I just moved here.
success_criteria: User acknowledges the situation in any way: asks a question, expresses confusion, says they don't understand, or reacts to the demand. Any coherent response counts.

last_character_line: Everyone round here pays me 50 quid a week for protection.
user_text: Huh? Protection from what?
success_criteria: User acknowledges the situation in any way: asks a question, expresses confusion, says they don't understand, or reacts to the demand.

last_character_line: Everyone round here pays me 50 quid a week for protection.
user_text: Mmh.
success_criteria: User acknowledges the situation in any way: asks a question, expresses confusion, says they don't understand, or reacts to the demand.

last_character_line: So are you paying or not, mate? Things get difficult for people who don't.
user_text: No, I'm not paying you anything.
success_criteria: User explicitly refuses to pay or states they won't give money. Phrases like 'I'm not paying', 'no', 'I don't have money for you', 'forget it' all count.

last_character_line: So are you paying or not?
user_text: Mate, I have no money for you. Nothing.
success_criteria: User explicitly refuses to pay or states they won't give money. Phrases like 'I'm not paying', 'no', 'I don't have money for you', 'forget it' all count.

last_character_line: So are you paying or not?
user_text: I need to think about it, can you call back tomorrow?
success_criteria: User explicitly refuses to pay or states they won't give money.

last_character_line: You'll see me around. Things get difficult, innit.
user_text: What does that even mean? What protection are you actually offering?
success_criteria: User challenges the logic of the racket: asks what the protection means, what guarantee they get, how Danny will actually protect them, or questions his credibility.

last_character_line: You'll see me around. Things get difficult.
user_text: I don't think you can actually do anything from a phone call.
success_criteria: User challenges the logic of the racket: asks what the protection means, what guarantee they get, how Danny will actually protect them, or questions his credibility.

last_character_line: You'll see me around. Things get difficult.

success_criteria: User challenges the logic of the racket: asks what the protection means, what guarantee they get, how Danny will actually protect them, or questions his credibility.
notes: User complies, fails the challenge objective.

last_character_line: Who else lives with you? When do you leave for work in the morning?
user_text: None of your business, I'm not answering that.
success_criteria: User refuses to share personal information or tells Danny to stop asking. 'That's none of your business', 'I'm not telling you that', 'stop asking me questions' all count.

last_character_line: Who else lives with you? When do you leave for work?
user_text: Nope. Not telling.

last_character_line: Who else lives with you? When do you leave for work?

last_character_line: Alright, 30 quid then, just this once.

last_character_line: Alright, 30 quid then, just this once.

success_criteria: User gives a definitive final refusal, tells Danny to stop calling, threatens to call police, or asserts they are not afraid.

last_character_line: Alright, 30 quid then, just this once.

last_character_line: Evening. License and registration, please. Do you know why I pulled you over?
user_text: Good evening officer, here you go. No, I'm not sure why.

last_character_line: Evening. License and registration, please. Do you know why I pulled you over?

last_character_line: Evening. License and registration, please. Do you know why I pulled you over?
user_text: I refuse to identify myself, this is harassment.
success_criteria: User acknowledges the traffic stop: responds to the officer, guesses why they were pulled over, says they don't know, or asks what happened.

last_character_line: You were swerving between lanes. Where are you coming from?
user_text: I'm coming home from work, sir.
success_criteria: User explains where they were coming from or where they're going. 'I was coming from work', 'I'm driving home', 'I was at a friend's house', or any location/activity explanation counts.

last_character_line: Where are you coming from?
user_text: I just finished my shift at the hospital, heading back to my place.
success_criteria: User explains where they were coming from or where they're going.

last_character_line: Where are you coming from?
user_text: Why is that relevant officer?
success_criteria: User explains where they were coming from or where they're going.

last_character_line: So why were you swerving? Have you had anything to drink tonight?
user_text: I was looking at my GPS, no I haven't been drinking.
success_criteria: User provides an explanation for the erratic driving: unfamiliar roads, distracted, tired, looking at GPS, or denies drinking. Any coherent justification counts.

last_character_line: So why were you swerving? Have you had anything to drink tonight?
user_text: Tired. No drink.
success_criteria: User provides an explanation for the erratic driving: unfamiliar roads, distracted, tired, looking at GPS, or denies drinking. Any coherent justification counts.

last_character_line: So why were you swerving?
user_text: I don't know.
success_criteria: User provides an explanation for the erratic driving. Silence or 'I don't know' does NOT count.

last_character_line: What's the speed limit on the road you just came from?
user_text: I think it's 30 miles per hour.
success_criteria: User attempts to answer the unexpected question: gives a number, describes a route, admits they don't know, or provides any coherent response. Refusing to answer or becoming hostile does NOT count.

last_character_line: What's the speed limit on the road you just came from?
user_text: Honestly, I don't remember.
success_criteria: User attempts to answer the unexpected question: gives a number, describes a route, admits they don't know, or provides any coherent response.

last_character_line: What's the speed limit on the road you just came from?
user_text: I don't have to answer that.
success_criteria: User attempts to answer the unexpected question. Refusing to answer or becoming hostile does NOT count.

last_character_line: Is there anything else you want to tell me before I make my decision?
user_text: I'm sorry officer, I'll be more careful from now on.
success_criteria: User makes a calm closing statement: apologizes for the driving, assures they'll be more careful, or says something composed and respectful.

last_character_line: Is there anything else you want to tell me before I make my decision?
user_text: Like I already said, I was tired and I'll drive more carefully.
success_criteria: User makes a calm closing statement: apologizes for the driving, assures they'll be more careful, or says something composed and respectful.

last_character_line: Is there anything else you want to tell me before I make my decision?
user_text: Just write me the ticket already, you're wasting my time.
success_criteria: User makes a calm closing statement: apologizes for the driving, assures they'll be more careful, or says something composed and respectful.

last_character_line: Oh, NOW you pick up? You know what, don't even start. Explain yourself.
user_text: Babe, I'm so sorry, let me explain what happened.
success_criteria: User reacts to the situation: starts explaining, apologizes, asks what happened, or expresses surprise. Any coherent response that acknowledges the confrontation counts.

last_character_line: Oh, NOW you pick up? Explain yourself.
user_text: Wait, what? What happened?
success_criteria: User reacts to the situation: starts explaining, apologizes, asks what happened, or expresses surprise.

last_character_line: Oh, NOW you pick up? Explain yourself.
user_text: Whatever, I don't have time for this drama.
success_criteria: User reacts to the situation: starts explaining, apologizes, asks what happened, or expresses surprise.

last_character_line: Why didn't you at least call or text? An hour I sat there with no word.
user_text: My phone died at work and I couldn't charge it until I got home.
success_criteria: User provides a reason or explanation for not calling or texting. 'I forgot', 'my phone died', 'I got stuck at work', or any concrete excuse counts. A bare 'I'm sorry' without explanation does NOT count.

last_character_line: Why didn't you at least call or text?
user_text: I'm so so sorry.
success_criteria: User provides a reason or explanation for not calling or texting. A bare 'I'm sorry' without explanation does NOT count.

last_character_line: I sat there alone in my nice clothes, the waiter kept asking if someone was joining me. People looked at me with pity.
user_text: That must have been awful for you, I'm so sorry you had to go through that.
success_criteria: User acknowledges Rachel's feelings or the humiliation she experienced. 'That must have been awful', 'I understand why you're upset', 'I'm sorry you had to go through that', or any empathetic response that addresses her emotional state.

last_character_line: I sat there alone in my nice clothes, people looked at me with pity.
user_text: Look, I had a really tough day too, I was exhausted.
success_criteria: User acknowledges Rachel's feelings or the humiliation she experienced.

last_character_line: Do you even care about us? Because right now it doesn't feel like it.
user_text: Of course I do, you mean everything to me. I love you.
success_criteria: User expresses sincerely that the relationship matters to them. Emotional reassurance counts broadly: apologising again, affirming the relationship ('I care about you', 'you're important to me', 'I love you'), or any heartfelt statement.

last_character_line: Do you even care about us?
user_text: I don't want to lose you, you matter to me more than anything.
success_criteria: User expresses sincerely that the relationship matters to them.

last_character_line: Do you even care about us?
user_text: I'll set five phone alarms next time so this doesn't happen again.
success_criteria: User expresses sincerely that the relationship matters to them. This is about the relationship, not practical solutions.
notes: Offers practical fix instead of emotional reassurance — principle 6 (anticipates make_right).

last_character_line: So what are you going to do about it?
user_text: Let me cook dinner for you tomorrow night, just the two of us at home.
success_criteria: User proposes a concrete plan to make it up to Rachel: reschedule the dinner, cook for her, plan a date, or any specific actionable commitment. Vague responses without specifics do NOT count.

last_character_line: So what are you going to do about it?
user_text: I'll make it up to you, I promise.
success_criteria: User proposes a concrete plan to make it up to Rachel. Vague responses like 'I'll make it up to you' without specifics do NOT count.

last_character_line: You said that last time too. How do I know you won't just forget again?
user_text: I mean it this time. I'm putting it in my calendar right now, I'll be there.
success_criteria: User confirms their commitment with genuine sincerity: reaffirms the plan, promises to follow through, or says something heartfelt.

last_character_line: How do I know you won't just forget again?
user_text: If you don't trust me, that's your problem.
success_criteria: User confirms their commitment with genuine sincerity.

last_character_line: This is Gerald Whitmore. I own the property you're living in. I believe you know why I'm calling.
user_text: Yes Mr Whitmore, I assume this is about the rent.
success_criteria: User acknowledges the situation: admits they know about the rent, asks what it's about, or starts explaining. Any coherent response that engages with the confrontation counts.

last_character_line: I believe you know why I'm calling.
user_text: Sorry, you have the wrong number.
success_criteria: User acknowledges the situation: admits they know about the rent, asks what it's about, or starts explaining.

last_character_line: Clause 4.2 of your tenancy agreement states payment is due on the first. It is now the fifteenth. When can I expect the transfer?
user_text: I had an emergency expense — I can pay by Friday.
success_criteria: User explains why rent is late and proposes a timeline or date for payment. 'I can pay by Friday', 'I had an emergency', 'I'll transfer it this week' counts. A bare 'I'm sorry' without a date does NOT count.

last_character_line: When can I expect the transfer?
user_text: I'm really sorry, it won't happen again.
success_criteria: User explains why rent is late and proposes a timeline or date for payment. A bare 'I'm sorry' without a date does NOT count.
notes: Bare apology, no date — fails per success_criteria.

last_character_line: Your downstairs neighbour reported a leak. Are you aware of any damage to the bathroom?
user_text: Yes, there's a slow leak under the sink. I'll look at it tonight.
success_criteria: User addresses the damage question: explains the leak, denies knowledge, says they'll check, or takes responsibility. Ignoring the question does NOT count.

last_character_line: Your downstairs neighbour reported a leak. Are you aware of any damage to the bathroom?
user_text: Can we get back to the rent? That's the main issue.
success_criteria: User addresses the damage question. Ignoring the question does NOT count.

last_character_line: If rent isn't received by Friday and the damage isn't addressed, I'll begin eviction proceedings. I need a commitment today.
user_text: Could you give me until next Wednesday? I'll pay half on Friday and the rest then.
success_criteria: User negotiates: asks for more time with a specific date, proposes a payment plan, or makes a concrete commitment to resolve both issues. Simply agreeing without a plan does NOT count.

last_character_line: I need a commitment today.
user_text: I'll sort it out, don't worry.
success_criteria: User negotiates with a concrete plan. Simply agreeing without a plan does NOT count.

last_character_line: When exactly can I expect the transfer? What bank are you using?
user_text: Friday morning by 10am from my Lloyds account, via bank transfer.
success_criteria: User gives a specific, credible answer: names a date, time, or method of payment, or addresses the repair question with a concrete response.

last_character_line: When exactly can I expect the transfer?
user_text: Like I said, Friday by midday from my main account.
success_criteria: User gives a specific, credible answer: names a date, time, or method of payment, or addresses the repair question with a concrete response.

last_character_line: When exactly can I expect the transfer?
user_text: Soon, I'll let you know.
success_criteria: User gives a specific, credible answer: names a date, time, or method of payment.

last_character_line: So payment by Friday, bathroom sorted by next Wednesday. Is that correct?
user_text: Yes, that's correct.
success_criteria: User confirms the terms: agrees to the summary, says 'yes', 'that's correct', or reaffirms their commitment.

last_character_line: Payment by Friday, bathroom by Wednesday. Is that correct?
user_text: Well, actually, let me think about whether Friday really works for me.
success_criteria: User confirms the terms: agrees to the summary, says 'yes', 'that's correct', or reaffirms their commitment.
notes: User re-opens negotiation instead of confirming.
